prompt:
Implement a Local Operations & Compliance Console for an offline-first education and field-services provider that manages service offerings, staff scheduling, content governance, and finance reconciliation in one place. The React-based web UI opens to a dashboard showing today's capacity, open exceptions, and moderation queues, with multi-condition filtering (category, tag, date range, status, staff member) and sortable tables that can be exported to CSV or Excel for offline sharing. Administrators maintain a service catalog where each service can be configured with duration (15-240 minutes in 15-minute increments), required headcount (1-10), required tools, and optional add-ons; pricing supports a base price in USD, tiered pricing by duration or headcount, and surcharges such as after-hours +20% and same-day booking +$25, while listing status and a daily order-taking volume cap prevent overbooking. Staffing coordinators manage a roster of proctors and field staff, track credentials and availability, and rely on auto-scheduling that blocks conflicts such as consecutive assignments with less than a 30-minute buffer or two assignments in the same session window; reassignment and backup flows require a reason code and a staff confirmation step. Operations reviewers use a moderation console for multi-level review and re-review of submitted profiles and posts, with rule-based auto-blocking for prohibited keywords and a gray-release option that exposes content only to internal roles for 24 hours before full publish; all actions show immediate UI feedback and are traceable from item history to operator identity.

The backend uses Go with Echo to provide REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled React frontend over local TLS, enabling the entire system to run on an on-prem workstation or LAN without internet dependencies. PostgreSQL persists tenants, users, roles, services, specs, pricing rules, capacity calendars, staff availability, schedules, resource master data, moderation decisions, and reconciliation cases; education resources support metadata models (subject, grade, type, attributes, tags), custom fields, relationships (dependency, substitute, bundle), and versioning with release notes, diff comparison, and one-click rollback limited to the last 10 versions. Access control enforces tenant isolation plus RBAC for standard roles (Administrator, Scheduler, Reviewer, Auditor) and ABAC rules such as restricting schedule edits to assigned locations; sensitive fields like bank account numbers are masked in the UI and stored encrypted at rest, with a local keyring and quarterly key rotation. Audit logs are immutable for critical actions including logins, permission changes, publishing, moderation edits, and resource version releases, retained for 7 years with configurable legal-hold and secure deletion workflows. Security protections include abnormal-login detection (more than 5 failed attempts in 15 minutes triggers a 30-minute lockout), on-box CAPTCHA after 3 failures, and API rate limiting per user to reduce abuse. Reconciliation supports importing bank or payment transaction feeds from CSV/Excel stored locally, automatically matching to internal records using amount, timestamp (+-10 minutes), counterparty account, and memo similarity, then producing exception lists for unmatched items, suspected duplicates, and variances over $1.00; each exception must be resolved via an assignment, disposition, and note, closing the loop with an auditable outcome.

Frontend stack
